title: 'Misleading financial statements': Suzlon to challenge Sebi's penalty order before SAT
summary: Suzlon Energy plans to challenge Sebi's order imposing penalties of nearly Rs 29 crore on the company and former executives over alleged accounting and disclosure violations related to FY14-FY18. The company will appeal before the Securities Appellate Tribunal, while Sebi maintains that certain transactions and disclosures misrepresented its financial position.

title: Zepto shares crash 30% in unlisted market despite Sebi nod for IPO. What's cooking?
summary: Zepto’s unlisted shares have dropped sharply despite Sebi approval for its IPO, signalling investor caution in a volatile market. The decline reflects broader weakness in pre-IPO valuations, macro uncertainty and funding pressures, even as the quick commerce firm prepares for a high-profile public issue amid intense competition.

title: Mcap of 7 of top-10 valued firms erodes by Rs 1.54 lakh cr; Reliance takes biggest hit
summary: In a holiday-shortened last week, the BSE benchmark Sensex dropped 639.61 points, or 0.84 per cent, and the NSE Nifty declined 171.55 points, or 0.72 per cent.

title: Why Indian investors need global exposure today
summary: With the rupee weakening and structural pressures from oil, capital outflows and inflation differentials persisting, Indian investors face currency risk in domestic-only portfolios. The article argues for global diversification, highlighting how dollar assets provide both return potential and currency protection, making international exposure a strategic necessity.

title: Market Outlook: US-Iran talks, crude oil among 5 factors to drive D-St in first week of June
summary: Markets brace for a crucial week. Investors watch US-Iran peace talks closely. Crude oil price drops also impact sentiment. Foreign investors sold heavily, impacting Indian equities. The Rupee shows strength against the US Dollar. Technical indicators suggest a lack of strong trend. Geopolitical and economic developments will drive market sensitivity.

title: Why do smart investors still lose money? Bernard Baruch’s guide to investing discipline
summary: Even seasoned investors lose money due to behavioural biases, poor timing, and overreaction to market noise. Drawing on Bernard Baruch’s principles, the piece highlights how discipline, patience, and rational decision-making matter more than intelligence in navigating volatility, avoiding emotional traps, and achieving long-term success in equity markets.

title: Bonus issues, dividends and more: Reliance Industries, Trent among 20 stocks turning ex-date this week
summary: Reliance Industries, Trent, and 18 other stocks are set to turn ex-date this week for corporate actions including dividends and bonus issues. Investors must hold shares by the record date to be eligible. Key events include Anand Rathi Wealth's 1:1 bonus and Trent's 1:2 bonus issue.

title: 20 months of bearish markets: One additional letter that tells you which 'cheap' stocks are actually cheap
summary: Most investors live by one ratio. That ratio that tells them all they want to know – or should one say all they want to hear? They open a screen, sort by it, and start picking from the bottom. It is the habit of a lifetime, and it is wrong more often than they realise. But add a single letter to the ratio and it changes which stocks look cheap, which look expensive, and which look like a trap. In a market that has gone nowhere for 20 months, getting that distinction right has rarely mattered more.

While some of the global triggers continue this week, read on to know what are the fresh factors to watch out for this week.

title: There will be investing opportunities when the Strait of Hormuz Reopens. Think ETFs.
summary: The war’s end would help China. Two quick ways to tap Chinese stocks: the iShares MSCI China ETF and the iShares MSCI China A-shares ETF.

title: SpaceX, OpenAI fundraising sparks investor hunt for Asia’s next AI winners beyond chipmaking giants
summary: Fresh fundraising and potential IPOs by OpenAI, SpaceX and Anthropic are driving investors to seek new AI opportunities across Asia. Bloomberg reported that firms involved in components, servers, packaging, connectivity and energy infrastructure could benefit from the next wave of AI spending.

title: Amazon may be bigger, but Zomato's Deepinder Goyal says Blinkit has the edge in quick commerce- Here's why
summary: Zomato founder Deepinder Goyal believes Blinkit has a better chance than Amazon of winning India’s quick-commerce battle. He highlighted Blinkit’s profitability, market share and expanding product range, while arguing that quick commerce requires a different model from traditional e-commerce.

title: Google techie bet $2.7 million on Polymarket with company secrets, won $1.2 million, then FBI caught him
summary: Michele Spagnuolo, 36, spent a decade earning awards and speaking invitations for his cybersecurity work at Google. Prosecutors now allege he used nonpublic data about the world's most-searched people to place $2.7 million in bets on Polymarket and pocket more than a million dollars in winnings

title: Two market manipulation stories, one lesson
summary: The market has never been a level playing field, and recent events have merely underscored how uneven it remains. The rational response is not to chase headlines, but to avoid playing a timing game

title: Crypto investing: From SIP-style investing to large-cap assets – How to fit Bitcoin, digital assets into your portfolio
summary: Retail investors remain cautious about crypto due to market volatility. Experts suggest a disciplined approach: invest regularly, focus on quality assets, and allocate 2-5% of portfolios to crypto for stability and growth potential.

summary: Market swings can test every investor, but avoiding common SIP investment mistakes in 2026 can improve mutual fund returns, strengthen financial goals and support long-term wealth creation. summary: Staying disciplined and aligned with financial goals is key to successful investing, rather than chasing short-term gains in fluctuating markets.
